target.name: Percepto
target.founder: Dor Abuhasira, Ariel Avitan, Sagi Blonder, Raviv Raz

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $128M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Tekever
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Percepto is a Series C autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The valuation universe comprises six private companies active in this space, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air; no public comparables exist in the narrowly defined peer set. Percepto has raised $128M to date across its funding rounds.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised ($128M) as a baseline proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C to subsequent-stage transitions yields an implied valuation of $218M. A peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting quality thresholds (fewer than three datapoints after filtering for stage and valuation tier), making sector-wide medians unreliably broad.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $185M to $250M, centered on $218M. This range reflects inherent uncertainty around appropriate step-up multiples for autonomous systems companies and the absence of tightly comparable reference transactions. The single-method result warrants caution; triangulation across additional market transactions or a broader peer set would strengthen conviction. Key variables include market sentiment toward autonomous platforms post-Series C and capital intensity relative to comparable funding rounds in adjacent subsectors.
